Refer to the API documentation for detailed endpoint specifications and authentication requirements. [FOR RAILS APPS, ADAPT AS NEEDED] ## 🔐 Authentication [IF USING BAAS, COPY THIS:] When authentication is enabled, the service supports two types of credentials: ### User Credentials If you have user credentials (email and password), use the following header: ``` Authorization: Basic users/: ``` ### Application Credentials If you have application credentials (public key and private key), use the following header: ``` Authorization: Basic apps/: ``` Both credential types use Basic Authentication encoding (base64). [IF USING OTHER AUTH, DESCRIBE IT] ## 🔗 External Services Integration The service integrates with multiple Bancard services. Service endpoints are configured in `config/services.yml` and `config/restool/` files (versioned in the `bancard-configurations` repository). The service communicates with: [LIST ALL SERVICES FROM config/services.yml:] - **[Service Name]** - [Description] **Note**: Service URLs and credentials in the sample configuration files are valid for staging environment, allowing you to run the service using staging services. ## 🏗️ Architecture ### Technology Stack - **Framework**: [FRAMEWORK NAME] (~> [VERSION]) - **Language**: Ruby [X.X.X] [IF JRUBY:]- **Production**: JRuby [VERSION] (for better performance and JVM integration) - **Database**: [MySQL/PostgreSQL] [VERSION] (via ActiveRecord with [ADAPTER]) - **Web Server**: [Puma/Unicorn] (~> [VERSION]) [IF BACKGROUND JOBS:]- **Background Jobs**: [Sidekiq/etc] with [Scheduler] (~> [VERSION]) [IF REDIS:]- **Caching & Job Queue**: Redis [VERSION] - **ORM**: ActiveRecord [VERSION] [ADD OTHER KEY TECHNOLOGIES] ### Key Dependencies The service relies on several categories of dependencies: #### Core Framework & Libraries [LIST FROM GEMFILE WITH VERSIONS:] - `gem-name` (~> version) - Description [IF APPLICABLE:] #### [Domain-Specific Category Name] [LIST DOMAIN GEMS] #### Bancard Internal Libraries [LIST ALL BANCARD GEMS:] - `bancard-picasso` - Core Bancard framework - `bancard-picasso-listing` - Listing features [etc...] #### Monitoring & Error Tracking [LIST MONITORING GEMS:] - `sentry-raven` (~> [VERSION]) - Error tracking and monitoring [IF OPENTELEMETRY:]- OpenTelemetry support for distributed tracing #### Development & Testing [LIST DEV/TEST GEMS:] - `rspec` (~> [VERSION]) - Testing framework - `factory_bot` (~> [VERSION]) - Test fixtures - `rubocop` (~> [VERSION]) - Code style checker - [other dev test gems] ## 📁 Project Structure [CREATE ASCII TREE BASED ON ACTUAL DIRECTORIES] Example for Angus: ``` project-name/ ├── bin/ # Executable scripts │ ├── setup # Development environment setup │ ├── dev # Development server launcher │ └── ... ├── config/ # Configuration files ├── db/ # Database files │ └── migrations/ # Database migrations ├── definitions/ # API endpoint definitions (Angus) ├── lib/ # Application libraries ├── models/ # ActiveRecord models ├── resources/ # API resource handlers (Angus) ├── spec/ # Test files └── README.md # This file ``` ## 🔧 Configuration ### Environment Setup The project uses sample configuration files that are copied during setup. Configuration files are located in the `config/` directory: [LIST ALL .sample FILES:] - `[filename].yml` - [Description] (copied from `[filename].sample.yml`) ### Environment Variables [LIST FROM CODE ANALYSIS:] - `VARIABLE_NAME` - [Description] (default: `[VALUE]`) ### Database Configuration The service uses [DATABASE TYPE] databases: - **Development**: `[project_name]_development` - **Test**: `[project_name]_test` - **Production**: `[project_name]_production` Database connections are configured in `config/database.yml`. The first time you run the setup, databases are created automatically. ## 🧪 Testing ### Running Tests ```bash # Run all tests bundle exec rspec # Run specific test file bundle exec rspec spec/[path]/[file]_spec.rb # Run tests with coverage COVERAGE=true bundle exec rspec ``` ### Code Quality ```bash # Run linter (Rubocop) bundle exec rubocop # Auto-fix violations bundle exec rubocop -a ``` [add brakeman if present] ## 🚀 Development Workflow ### Starting the Application ```bash # Start development server (uses bin/dev) ./bin/dev ``` The development server runs on port [PORT] by default. You can change this by setting the `PORT` environment variable. [IF BACKGROUND JOBS EXIST:] ### Background Jobs [Sidekiq/etc] is used for background job processing: ```bash # Start [Sidekiq/etc] worker ./bin/[COMMAND] ``` [IF SCHEDULER:] [Job system] also includes a scheduler for recurring tasks. Configuration is in `config/[CONFIG-FILE].yml`. ### Database Operations ```bash # To run all the migrations bin/setup # Or manually bundle exec rake db:migrate # Run migrations for test environment [BANCARD_SERVICES_ENV or RAILS_ENV]=test bundle exec rake db:migrate # Check migration status bundle exec rake db:migrate:status # Rollback last migration bundle exec rake db:rollback ``` [IF APPLICABLE:] The setup script automatically runs migrations for both development and test environments only if the database host is configured as `db` (Docker setup). ## 📊 Monitoring & Logging ### Logging Logging is configured via `config/loggers.yml`. The service uses `bancard-loggers` for structured logging. Logs are typically written to the `log/` directory. ### Error Tracking [LIST ERROR TRACKING:] - **Sentry** integration via `sentry-raven` gem for error monitoring and exception tracking - Configure Sentry DSN in your environment or configuration files ## 🚀 Deployment ### Docker Deployment Deployment is handled through the `bancard-docker` project. See more details at: https://github.com/bancardsa/bancard-docker The service is containerized and can be deployed using Docker. The base image is defined in `docker/Dockerfile.base`. ### Production Considerations - All application configuration, including external services configuration, is versioned in the `bancard-configurations` repository [IF JRUBY:] - Use JRuby [VERSION] for production ## 🆘 Support For technical support or questions, contact the development team. --- **Note**: This service is part of the Bancard payment ecosystem and depends on several internal Bancard gems and services. Ensure you have access to the required private repositories and service endpoints before setting up the development environment. --- END OF README TEMPLATE --- ## FORMATTING RULES: 1.
